scope_description: Pre-release review batch: doctor-config-surface (high-risk, 5 cells), readme-demo-bind-restore (tiny, 2 cells), windows-username-length-fix (tiny, 1 cell). Cumulative diff is cell-scoped (not a raw commit-range diff) because these commits interleave with cross-platform-install and readme-rewrite commits that touch overlapping files (README.md) and are out of scope.

excluded.reason: already reviewed and merge-approved today via review-cross-platform-install-20260720
not requested by user for this review batch; touches README.md but predates and is disjoint from readme-demo-bind-restore's restoration commit
no-op bookkeeping closure, zero product files touched, nothing to review
cell m1-6-integration dropped as superseded/stale, zero product files touched

head: 09991fbf9c4bbc0c529087d492676b63e891e006

reviewers.trigger: external herdr socket dependency checks with skip-on-failure paths, external-provider (github/telegram) token file handling in secrets.rs

findings.title: default_config_json duplicates config::ensure_config's default values as a second source of truth
findings.evidence: src/doctor/checks.rs default_config_json (~L2047-2064) hand-writes bind_addr/protocol/poll/static_dir defaults that mirror ensure_config's; own comment concedes the duplication
findings.impact: If ensure_config's defaults (e.g. the pinned herdr_protocol wire-compat number) change, the unparseable-config backup-then-recreate path silently writes a stale default, in the exact branch that fires after data has already been backed up as unrecoverable.

findings.title: default_config_json duplicates config::ensure_config's default values as a second source of truth
findings.evidence: src/doctor/checks.rs default_config_json (~L2047-2064) hand-writes bind_addr/protocol/poll/static_dir defaults that mirror ensure_config's; own comment concedes the duplication
findings.impact: If ensure_config's defaults (e.g. the pinned herdr_protocol wire-compat number) change, the unparseable-config backup-then-recreate path silently writes a stale default, in the exact branch that fires after data has already been backed up as unrecoverable.

findings.title: Settings editor offers no way to remove or narrow an allowed_roots entry — append-only
findings.evidence: src/doctor/edit.rs edit_allowed_roots (~L2794-2826) and the guided-fix menu (~L1916-1968) only ever add a root, never remove/replace one
findings.impact: After a user widens allowed_roots via D9's typed confirmation (e.g. to '/'), doctor provides no path to narrow it back — contradicts the 'single friendly surface to edit any setting' framing (D1) for this one field. Advisory: a product-scope question, not a locked-decision violation.

findings.title: Secrets::from_env performs file I/O and prints raw stderr diagnostics, diverging from the app's tracing-based logging
findings.evidence: src/config/mod.rs from_env() now also reads herdr-go.env (~L1229-1236); src/config/secrets.rs read_trusted_env_file uses eprintln! on a protection failure (~L1382-1388) instead of tracing::warn! used elsewhere (e.g. main.rs's non-loopback notice)
findings.impact: An operator running under a log collector that captures tracing output but not raw stderr misses the 'ignoring herdr-go.env' warning entirely; the method name from_env also now misdescribes what it does.

findings.title: Non-loopback bind warning (D16) fires in the settings editor but not on the guided config-repair path
findings.evidence: src/doctor/edit.rs edit_config_field checks !addr.ip().is_loopback() before persisting (~L2779-2785); src/doctor/checks.rs apply_field_repairs has no equivalent branch (~L1875-1881)
findings.impact: Repairing a malformed bind_addr through the guided config-fix flow (as opposed to the settings editor) can widen the bind to non-loopback with no D16 warning shown, even though it is also 'the moment bind_addr is changed.'

findings.title: Guided 'recreate default config' can set allowed_roots to the home directory with no D9 breadth confirmation
findings.evidence: src/doctor/checks.rs default_config_json (~L2051-2057) falls back to the home directory when ~/projects doesn't exist; written via backup_and_recreate with no breadth prompt
findings.impact: On a fresh host without ~/projects, accepting the unparseable-config recreate offer grants the gateway access to the whole home directory with no D9 confirmation, though this mirrors config::ensure_config's existing first-run default rather than being a new regression.

findings.title: Settings editor blames the field the user just edited when a *different* pre-existing field is what fails validation
findings.evidence: src/doctor/edit.rs persist_field's RepairOutcome::StillInvalid arm always prints '{label} value is invalid' using the just-edited field's label, regardless of which field(s) invalid_fields actually names (~L2856-2868)
findings.impact: A user editing a valid field while an unrelated field is already invalid sees a misleading error blaming their (correct) edit, sending them looking for a bug that isn't where the message claims.

findings.title: default_config_json builds JSON via Rust's {:?} Debug formatting instead of a real serializer
findings.evidence: src/doctor/checks.rs default_config_json interpolates the allowed-root path with {:?} (~L2058-2063) instead of serde_json encoding
findings.impact: Debug escaping diverges from JSON string escaping for control characters/some Unicode in a home path, which can produce an invalid document that Config::load_str then rejects — failing the exact 'recreate a working default' operation the user asked for, after the corrupted file was already backed up.

findings.title: read_trusted_env_file stores an untrimmed secret value while emptiness is judged on the trimmed form
findings.evidence: src/config/secrets.rs read_trusted_env_file inserts v.to_string() untrimmed (~L1396-1400); resolve_from_env_and_file filters on !v.trim().is_empty() but returns the untrimmed clone (~L1360-1365)
findings.impact: A hand-edited herdr-go.env line like 'KEY= value' resolves to a token with a leading space, causing silent auth failures against external providers with no visible cause (the value itself is never printed by design).

findings.title: replace_or_append's documented duplicate-collapsing behavior is untested
findings.evidence: src/config/secrets.rs replace_or_append dedup logic (~L1336-1345); every existing test reaches it only via write_secret_in, which never produces a file with a pre-existing raw duplicate key
findings.impact: D10's exact reason for existing (the legacy ensure_web_secret only appends, so a real file can carry a duplicate key) is unexercised by any test; a regression that keeps the last match or stops deduping would go undetected.

findings.title: offer_config_fix's unrecognized-field cleanup branch (drops an unknown field) is untested
findings.evidence: src/doctor/checks.rs offer_config_fix's deny_unknown_fields repair branch (~L1838-1852) has no covering test
findings.impact: This branch drops data on a security-adjacent invariant ('tokens never live in config.json'); a regression that persists the unknown field instead of dropping it (e.g. a stray github_token) would go undetected.

findings.title: allowed_roots breadth guard bypassable by trivial path spelling (no normalization before classification)
findings.evidence: src/config/write.rs classify_root_breadth (~L843-860); src/doctor/checks.rs prompt_new_allowed_root (~L1977-1997)
findings.impact: A trailing slash, '/.', or '..' segment makes an over-broad allowed_roots entry (home dir, filesystem root, symlink) classify as Narrow and skip D9's typed-confirmation guard, silently widening the web-exposed gateway's filesystem access.

findings.title: The startup non-loopback warning call site in main.rs has no test coverage after extraction to a shared helper
findings.evidence: src/main.rs calls herdr_go::doctor::non_loopback_bind_warning inside an is_loopback() guard (~L3094-3105); only the editor's call to the same helper is tested
findings.impact: A refactor that drops or inverts the startup is_loopback() guard would bind publicly with no warning printed, and no test would fail.

findings.title: write_secret reports failure to the user after the secret value is already committed to disk
findings.evidence: src/config/secrets.rs write_secret_in renames the temp file into place, then calls validate_token_protection as the function's returned result (~L1320-1324); callers report any Err as 'could not save'
findings.impact: If the post-rename permission check ever fails, the user is told the save failed while the secret is in fact already live in herdr-go.env and will load at next start — state and report disagree.

findings.title: Startup can print a false 'ignoring herdr-go.env' warning under systemd even when every secret already comes from the process env
findings.evidence: src/config/secrets.rs resolve_from_env_and_file reads and permission-checks the env file unconditionally before applying env-first precedence (~L1357-1372)
findings.impact: Under systemd (which injects herdr-go.env via EnvironmentFile= regardless of file mode), a non-600 file logs a misleading 'being ignored' warning on every start even though its contents are irrelevant because the process env already supplies everything.

findings.title: Temp files from interrupted writes (including one holding a secret) are never cleaned up
findings.evidence: src/config/secrets.rs write_secret_in cleans up on returned Err but not on a mid-write crash between temp-write and rename; src/config/write.rs write_atomic has no cleanup on any failure branch (~L607-629)
findings.impact: A process kill between writing the temp file and the atomic rename leaves an orphaned .tmp-<rand> file behind permanently; for the secrets writer this is an unmanaged, owner-only copy of a live token with no reaper.

findings.title: herdr ping has no evidenced read timeout; phase-3's unconditional full re-run doubles exposure to a hang
findings.evidence: src/doctor/mod.rs phase-3 re-run (~L2205-2207) calls build_checks().await a second time, re-invoking client.ping().await (checks.rs ~L1707); src/herdr/socket.rs (the actual ping/timeout implementation) was outside this review's read scope so this is not fully confirmed
findings.impact: If herdr accepts a connection but never responds, doctor could hang with no upper bound, and D14's added re-run now does this twice per interactive session. Flagged for confirmation against src/herdr/socket.rs before treating as settled.

findings.title: Concurrent secret writes to herdr-go.env silently lose updates (no locking on read-modify-write)
findings.evidence: src/config/secrets.rs write_secret_in (~L1309-1325); read-modify-rename with no lock, races with startup's append-based ensure_web_secret
findings.impact: A concurrent doctor session and the gateway's own startup secret generation can race; the losing writer's key silently disappears, e.g. an already-generated web secret can be dropped, invalidating live sessions on next restart.

findings.title: D14 socket-skip behavior is tested only via synthetic Check structs, not the real build_checks() builder
findings.evidence: src/doctor/checks.rs build_checks() restructure (~L1653-1738); only tests are all_ok_treats_a_skipped_critical_check_as_non_blocking and render_report_marks_skipped_distinctly, both on hand-built Check structs
findings.impact: A future refactor could re-introduce the early-return this feature deliberately removed (or drop the skipped-check pushes) and every existing test would stay green — the exact D14 regression would go undetected.

findings.title: Non-interactive / --check write-suppression gate in run() has no test coverage
findings.evidence: src/doctor/mod.rs run() early-return guard (~L2185-2187) and the phase-4 editor block (~L3069-3087); no test drives the {--check, piped, interactive} matrix
findings.impact: D5/D15's unattended-mutation prohibition (decision 4827aae8) is a security-relevant contract enforced by a code path with zero test coverage; a refactor could make --check or a piped invocation reach the fix/edit prompts with no test failing.

findings.title: Secret-resolution unit tests mutate shared process env vars in parallel without serialization (flaky by construction)
findings.evidence: src/config/secrets.rs process_env_wins_over_env_file and src/config/mod.rs secrets_absent_from_env_and_file_are_none both set/remove the same HERDR_GO_* env keys with no lock; cargo test runs threads in parallel
findings.impact: Two tests asserting opposite states of the same global env var can interleave under cargo test's default parallelism, producing intermittent, non-reproducible CI failures unrelated to any real defect.

findings.title: Windows username-length regression guard lives only in the capped cell's one-shot verify, wired into no recurring test suite
findings.evidence: .bee/cells/windows-username-length-fix-1.json verify field (python3 -c one-liner asserting prefix+8<=20); not added to tests/rename_contract.sh or any Pester test
findings.impact: The exact regression class this fix closed (a renamed identity prefix pushing New-LocalUser's generated username over 20 chars) has no standing, always-run guard; the next rename of that prefix reproduces the original blind spot, catchable only by a gated windows-2022 runner.

findings.title: Socket-resolution failure prints two report lines both labelled 'herdr endpoint' (one fail, one skipped) — corroborated independently by 3 reviewers (architecture, code-quality, reliability)
findings.evidence: src/doctor/checks.rs: resolution Err branch pushes Check::fail("herdr endpoint", ...) (~L1656-1665); check #4's None branch pushes Check::skipped("herdr endpoint", ...) (~L1684-1687) with the identical label
findings.impact: Every doctor run against an unresolvable herdr socket shows a contradictory-looking duplicate line (one ✗, one ○, same label), undercutting the report clarity D14 was meant to deliver. Promoted from P3 to P2 per synthesis rule: independent corroboration across 3 reviewers promotes severity one level.

findings.title: Module split dropped fix.rs; guided-fix mutation logic lives in checks.rs behind a stale 'unchanged read-only' doc comment
findings.evidence: src/doctor/checks.rs header still reads 'moved here unchanged ... no logic or output-text changes' while the same file now holds ~330 lines of config-mutating guided-fix code; plan.md's Module structure section committed to a separate fix.rs
findings.impact: A maintainer auditing where doctor mutates config.json would be misled by the file's own doc comment into skipping it. Note: the binding cell-level must_haves (doctor-config-surface-4/-5 artifacts) never required fix.rs as a separate file, so this is plan-vs-implementation drift, not a missing required artifact.

findings.title: Guided-fix dispatch routes by free-form check label string with a silent no-op fallthrough, unlinked to the label's producer
findings.evidence: src/doctor/checks.rs offer_fixes matches check.label.as_str() against "config"/"allowed roots"/"web token" literals with a _ => {} default (~L1757-1768); build_checks defines those labels elsewhere with nothing linking producer and consumer
findings.impact: Rewording a fixable check's display label silently disables its guided fix with no compile error and no failing test, for two security-relevant fixes (allowed_roots widening, web-token provisioning).

uat.item: D1: doctor diagnoses, offers guided fixes, then ends with 'edit a setting? [y/N]'
D9: adding an over-broad allowed_roots entry (/ or home dir) requires explicit typed confirmation, not a plain y/n
D13: secret entry is masked (no echo) and confirms with length + last-3-chars after entry
uat.note: User initially reported no confirmation visible; reproduced independently via pty (orchestrator) and confirmation printed correctly. User confirmed root cause was output running together on their screen without a line break, not a product defect.

uat.item: D15: doctor --check is diagnose-only, no prompts, no writes
uat.note: Verified directly by orchestrator: cargo run -- doctor --check printed report only, exit 0, no product files modified (git status showed only pre-existing .bee/ bookkeeping changes unrelated to this run).

uat.item: D16: changing bind_addr to a non-loopback address in the settings editor prints the security warning immediately, before persisting

gate4.approved_by: bypass (full) — P1=0 and all 5 UAT items confirmed pass by user
